reasoning: The market asks if OpenAI will announce it has achieved AGI by December 31, 2025. This is defined by OpenAI as "Level 5" on their scale (an AI that can do the work of an entire organization). As of August 2025, OpenAI released GPT-5, and CEO Sam Altman explicitly clarified that while it shows signs of broad intelligence, it lacks the ability to learn and improve on its own, which is a key component of AGI, calling it just "a significant step along the path to AGI." The DevDay developer conference took place on October 6, 2025, launching GPT-5 Pro API and AgentKit, but importantly, no AGI claim was made. On their 5-level scale to AGI, these agents represent Level 3. It is mathematically and scientifically highly improbable that OpenAI will advance from Level 3 to Level 5, evaluate the model safely, and officially announce it as AGI in the next two months. The Polymarket estimate was at 4% on Oct 16, and there have been no breakthrough announcements between then and the Oct 26 knowledge cutoff. I assign a very low probability of 2%.
